zh.lifeSummary: 當James Butler 1st Earl of Ormond於1304年10月17日出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 維其可洛福維其可洛福時，他的父親Sir Edmund Butler Earl Of Carrick33歲，母親Joan Fitz Gerald Countess Of Carrick23歲。他與Eleanor de Bohun結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1328年在英國英格蘭維爾爾索爾茲伯里被列為sir knight。他去世於1338年1月6日在愛爾蘭庫迪 基爾堅尼高萊安享年33歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭庫迪 基爾堅尼高萊安。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当James Butler 1st Earl of Ormond于1304年10月17日出生在爱尔兰庫迪 維其可洛福維其可洛福时，他的父亲Sir Edmund Butler Earl Of Carrick33岁，母亲Joan Fitz Gerald Countess Of Carrick23岁。他与Eleanor de Bohun结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他于1328年在英国英格兰維爾爾索爾茲伯里被列为sir knight。他去世于1338年1月6日在爱尔兰庫迪 基爾堅尼高萊安享年33岁，埋葬在爱尔兰庫迪 基爾堅尼高萊安。

nameMeanings.name: Butler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from a word that originally denoted a wine steward, usually the chief servant of a medieval household, from Norman French butuiller (Old French bouteillier, Latin buticularius, from buticula ‘bottle’). In the large households of royalty and the most powerful nobility, the title came to denote an officer of high rank and responsibility, only nominally concerned with the supply of wine, if at all. As well as being widespread in England, this is also the surname of an important Irish family, descended from Theobald FitzWalter, who was appointed Chief Butler of Ireland by King Henry II in 1177. It is Gaelicized as de Buitléir.

English: occasionally perhaps an occupational name from Middle English boteler ‘maker of bottles (usually of leather)’, a derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bo(u)teille ‘bottle’ and synonymous with Botelmaker.
Americanized form of French   Bouthillier   (see   Bouteiller  ).

variants.name: Buttler
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Butler  .
German: occupational name for a village tavern owner, from French bouteillier ‘butler’.
Americanized form of German Buttlar: habitational name from a place so named in Thuringia.

variants.name: Bouthillier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Jacques, Normand, Philippe, Yves.
French: variant of   Bouteiller  . This name is sometimes Americanized as   Butler  . Compare   Boutilier  .

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: When James Butler 1st Earl of Ormond was born on 17 October 1304, in Wicklow, County Wicklow, Ireland, his father, Sir Edmund Butler Earl Of Carrick, was 33 and his mother, Joan Fitz Gerald Countess Of Carrick, was 23. He married Eleanor de Bohun. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. In 1328, at the age of 24, his occupation is listed as sir knight in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom. He died on 6 January 1338, in Gowran, County Kilkenny, Ireland, at the age of 33, and was buried in Gowran, County Kilkenny, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque James Butler 1st Earl of Ormond est né le 17 octobre 1304, à Wicklow, County Wicklow, Irlande, son père, Sir Edmund Butler Earl Of Carrick, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Joan Fitz Gerald Countess Of Carrick, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Eleanor de Bohun. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. En 1328, à l’âge de 24 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est sir knight à Salisbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé le 6 janvier 1338, à Gowran, County Kilkenny, Irlande, à l’âge de 33 ans, et a été enterré à Gowran, County Kilkenny, Irlande.

de.lifeSummary: Als James Butler 1st Earl of Ormond am 17. Oktober 1304 in Wicklow, County Wicklow, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Edmund Butler Earl Of Carrick 33 und seine Mutter Joan Fitz Gerald Countess Of Carrick 23. Er hat Eleanor de Bohun geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. 1328, als er 24 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als sir knight in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom aufgeführt. Er starb am 6. Januar 1338 im Alter von 33 Jahren in Gowran, County Kilkenny, Irland und wurde in Gowran, County Kilkenny, Irland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando James Butler 1st Earl of Ormond è nato il il 17 ottobre 1304 nel Wicklow, County Wicklow, Irlanda, il suo padre, Sir Edmund Butler Earl Of Carrick, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Joan Fitz Gerald Countess Of Carrick, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Eleanor de Bohun. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie. Nel 1328, all'età di 24 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come sir knight nel Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì il 6 gennaio 1338, nel Gowran, County Kilkenny, Irlanda, all'età di 33 anni, e fu sepolto a Gowran, County Kilkenny, Irlanda.

ja.lifeSummary: James Butler 1st Earl of Ormondが1304年10月17日にアイルランドコウントイ ウィックロウウィックロウで生まれた時，父Sir Edmund Butler Earl Of Carrickは33歳，母Joan Fitz Gerald Countess Of Carrickは23歳であった。Eleanor de Bohunと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。1328年，24歳の時，イギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリーでの職業はsir knightと記録されている。彼は1338年1月6日にアイルランドコウントイ キルケニーゴウランで33歳で死去し，アイルランドコウントイ キルケニーゴウランにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: James Butler 1st Earl of Ormond(이)가 1304년 10월 17일 아일랜드 카운티 윅로 윅로에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Edmund Butler Earl Of Carrick(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Fitz Gerald Countess Of Carrick(은)는 23세였다. 그는 Eleanor de Bohun(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1328년, 24세의 나이로 그의 직업은 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 살리스버이에서 sir knight이었다. 그는 1338년 1월 6일 아일랜드 카운티 킬켄이 가란에서 33세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드 카운티 킬켄이 가란에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando James Butler 1st Earl of Ormond nasceu em 17 de outubro de 1304, em Wicklow, County Wicklow, Irlanda, seu pai, Sir Edmund Butler Earl Of Carrick, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Joan Fitz Gerald Countess Of Carrick, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Eleanor de Bohun. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Em 1328, com 24 anos, sua profissão aparece como sir knight em Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu em 6 de janeiro de 1338, em Gowran, County Kilkenny, Irlanda, com 33 anos, e foi sepultado em Gowran, County Kilkenny, Irlanda.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда James Butler 1st Earl of Ormond родился 17 октября 1304 г. в местности викклоу, коунти викклоу, Ирландия, его отцу по имени Sir Edmund Butler Earl Of Carrick было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Joan Fitz Gerald Countess Of Carrick, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Eleanor de Bohun. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. В 1328 году, в возрасте 24 лет,  он работал по профессии sir knight в местности салисбури, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер 6 января 1338 г. в местности гоуран, коунти килкенни, Ирландия в возрасте 33 лет и был похоронен в местности гоуран, коунти килкенни, Ирландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando James Butler 1st Earl of Ormond nació el 17 de octubre de 1304, en Wicklow, County Wicklow, Irlanda, su papá, Sir Edmund Butler Earl Of Carrick, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Joan Fitz Gerald Countess Of Carrick, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Eleanor de Bohun. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. En 1328, a la edad de 24 años, su ocupación registrada era sir knight en Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió el 6 de enero de 1338, en Gowran, County Kilkenny, Irlanda, a la edad de 33 años, y fue sepultado en Gowran, County Kilkenny, Irlanda.
